title: Founding Engineer — CAD Kernel
description: Own the geometry and solver surface of the AI CAD Copilot. You will design the sketch-constraint interface, integrate with a kernel or roll our own primitives, and make sure the copilot's outputs are geometry an engineer can actually manufacture.
responsibilities: Design the sketch and feature tool surface the copilot calls
Build the constraint-solver integration and topology inspector
Instrument silent failure modes (degenerate faces, non-manifold edges)
Partner with the AI team on the neural-symbolic loop
Define what a shippable geometry output looks like
requirements: 5+ years shipping CAD, EDA, CAE, or graphics kernels
Fluency in a boundary-representation kernel (Parasolid, ACIS, OCCT)
Comfort reading academic geometry papers and turning them into code
Track record of numerically-robust engineering (epsilons, tolerances, degeneracies)

title: Founding Engineer — AI Systems
description: Own the copilot's AI runtime end to end. You will design the agent loop, structure prompts for cache efficiency, build the eval harness before we build features, and make sure the deterministic verifier is the boss of the generative proposer.
responsibilities: Design and maintain the agent runtime, tool dispatcher, and budget guardrails
Build a real evaluation harness before shipping user-facing features
Instrument cost, latency, and correctness — all three at once
Own the neural-symbolic loop with the CAD kernel engineer
Choose when to defer to a stronger model and when to route to a smaller one
requirements: 4+ years shipping AI-powered products, not just prototypes
Deep familiarity with agent runtimes, tool use, and structured outputs
Fluency in prompt caching and inference cost economics
Willingness to build the boring evaluation infrastructure first

title: Product Designer — Design Tools
description: Design the surfaces of a copilot that lives inside another designer's workflow. You will shape how sketches, suggestions, and confidence indicators feel — under the strict latency budget of a real design tool.
responsibilities: Design the copilot's interaction surface end to end
Prototype latency-aware micro-interactions and streaming states
Build and maintain the studio's design system
Partner with engineering on the three-tier responsive architecture
requirements: 3+ years designing complex professional tools (CAD, EDA, IDE, DAW, design systems)
Fluency with tokens, motion, and system-level UI patterns
Comfort designing for engineers, not consumers
Portfolio that shows depth, not breadth

title: Mechanical Design Consultant
description: Bring the working machinist's phone call into the copilot. You will help us encode the process-capability knowledge that lives in DFM handbooks and shop-floor experience, and challenge the AI's outputs from the perspective of the engineer who has to manufacture them.
responsibilities: Review AI-generated geometries against real manufacturing constraints
Encode DFM rules for milling, molding, casting, and additive processes
Sit in on user research with mechanical design teams
Help write the domain sections of the eval set
requirements: Strong DFM instincts across at least two manufacturing processes
Willingness to work part-time with a small AI-first team
